findings.title: Venue allowlist accepts arbitrary executeLeg contracts under typed venue families
findings.attack_path: If governance or deployment enables an attacker-controlled address for any venue family, the router treats it as a trusted venue, approves it for the route-local ERC20 amount at src/FameRouter.sol:193, and dispatches to its generic executeLeg hook instead of a real venue ABI. That target can pull the approved user funds or receive native callValue while still sitting behind a seemingly typed Solidly/Uniswap/Slipstream/Universal family flag.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace generic executeLeg adapters with per-family constrained calldata decoders and calls to the actual audited venue/router ABIs, and make deployment validation prove each enabled target is the expected production contract for its family.

findings.title: Final minAmountOut can pass even when tokenOut transfer delivers less to the recipient
findings.attack_path: The router computes grossAmountOut from its own pre-settlement balance delta and checks minAmountOutAfterFee before transferring tokenOut. A fee-on-transfer or malicious ERC20 tokenOut can reduce the fee or recipient transfer at src/FameRouter.sol:108-111, so the transaction succeeds while the recipient receives less than the advertised post-fee minimum.
findings.suggested_fix: Either restrict route fixtures to final tokens with standard transfer semantics or enforce settlement by measuring recipient and fee-recipient balance deltas after transfer and reverting when the delivered recipient delta is below minAmountOutAfterFee.
